faq.question: Who should participate?
faq.answer:
Students and young leaders from 18-35 years old, from across Asia (South, Central, East, South East) and the Middle East who are passionate about their Christian faith, intent on learning more about missions and what God is doing around the world.

In order to participate in Arise Asia, potential participants must be recommended by a church leader from their country or by a member of a partnering organization listed in the About Us section. Once they have completed their application and provided two character references, their participation will be considered. Upon meeting the criteria, they will be invited to register and join us at the event. See criteria here.

faq.question: Is the conference in-person or also virtual?
faq.answer: The conference is in-person. In the future, we may decide to open the program up to virtual, depending on interest and inability to attend in-person.

faq.question: Why should people attend?
faq.answer:
This conference is for younger leaders from across Asia to be inspired about the great commission of what God is doing across Asia and in the world, hear fresh ideas for missions and take the next step to go where there currently is no Gospel.

They will understand advancements in missions movements and prayerfully consider their own contribution, locally and globally. They will also enjoy incredible ways to interact with others who are as passionate about their faith in God.

faq.question: What can people expect?
faq.answer:
Participants can expect to learn from experienced global missions practitioners from across Asia, with plenty of opportunities to ask questions from our speakers, plus evening cafés to connect with each other.

The program will maximize participant engagement through innovative lab sessions. Seminars will address critical missions issues that we face in the world, including how to prepare to go into missions, internship opportunities, the persecuted church, discipleship, spiritual and mental health, church planting, unreached people groups, international development, business as mission, justice issues and more.

nominationCriteria.description: As our Target group is within the age range of 18-35 years, we need our selection criteria for each country to have a young mission-oriented focus. The candidates must be within the age range of 18-35 years old.

As disciples of Jesus, our growing maturity and character should be shaped in the likeness of Christ. The candidate must have a striving character that the Scriptures embody every believer to strive for.

The goal of the conference to empower people towards missions, the candidates elected must be a dedicated member of a local church, committed, serving, and involved. As there will be candidates from various denominations applying, the goal is that the candidates elected must have a good grounding on basic doctrines of the faith and must have a sound understanding of the Bible.
The candidate must be an active member of a local church community, actively involved and striving towards serving the needs of the local community within the body of the church.
The candidate must have a strong conviction that the Lord is drawing him/her towards the call of being mobilized within the world and body of Christ to proclaim the Gospel.
The candidate must be a born-again believer in the saving work of our Lord Jesus, must-have put his trust in the Gospel and is a committed follower of Jesus Christ.
We aim to achieve a 50/50 balance of men and women at the congress.

nominationCriteria.criterion: Influence/Heart for Global Body of Christ
nominationCriteria.description: The selected candidate should be a person with influence in his/her local church, having served their community well, with a heart to influence the global body of Christ.

Cultural personalities also vary from continent to continent and from country to country. The selected candidate must have the ability to empathetically enter the culture where they will minister, hence the need for good communications skills.
The candidate must have experience and understanding of cross-cultural missions, and current mission practices and trends, or a strong inclination towards missions.

The candidate must have a heart for missions, should have some inclination towards seeing the global church mobilized for missions. Leaders of ministries (Evangelism, children, Bible distribution, church planters, hospitality) could all be potential candidates for the mission conference.

Prospective missionaries prepare to serve by strengthening their physical, mental, and emotional health. The prospective candidates must have exhibited sound physical, emotional, and spiritual health, before being recommended to the conference.
The candidate must be a man/woman of prayer. The selection committee should vitally check the prayer habits of the prospective candidates.

Mission calls include an assignment to teach the Gospel, support the work of Church departments or units or serve in the local community. The candidate must exhibit a servant heart to be willing to serve the local body under any assignment he/she is mobilized to.

Missionaries must be effective teachers of the Word of God. Teaching a Christian worldview as revealed by God in scripture is integral to the missionary task. We should, therefore, send missionaries to foreign mission fields who have taught the Gospel to unbelievers in their own culture and have empathetically nurtured new disciples to Christian maturity. The candidate must be an individual who is committed to teaching and proclaiming the Gospel faithfully in their area of influence.
The candidate must have developed some abilities or giftings in their current area of influence that would be used as they minister in the mission field.
The candidate must have a good testimony in his/her local church, with the church supporting the character and moral standing of the selected candidate.
